Cuvées

Beaujolais L'Ancien

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Elegant

Domaine Terres Dorées (Jean-Paul Brun)Beaujolais€€€€€€Still Red

Jean-Paul Brun's signature Beaujolais AOC uses old-vine, whole-cluster Gamay from the golden limestone soils of Pierres Dorées for a richer, more textured and age-worthy expression than typical Beaujolais.

Cuvée page →

Beaujolais-Villages

3.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Château de PizayBeaujolais€€€€€€Still Red

Château de Pizay's estate Beaujolais-Villages: more structured than typical Villages due to the estate's Morgon-adjacent soils, with a firmer edge and cleaner minerality.

Cuvée page →

Beaujolais-Villages Domaine du Comte

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Domaine Karim VionnetBeaujolais€€€€€€Still Red

Karim Vionnet's estate Beaujolais-Villages from the Domaine du Comte parcels: darker-fruited and more structured than a typical Villages, showing the producer's Morgon-influenced approach to low-intervention Gamay.

Cuvée page →

Brouilly

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Château de la ChaizeBeaujolais€€€€€€Still Red

Château de la Chaize's estate Brouilly from its historic 220-hectare Odenas property: full-flavoured, traditionally structured Gamay with ripe red fruit and reliable house style.

Brouilly

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Maison Georges DuboeufBeaujolais€€€€€€Still Red

Duboeuf's Brouilly from selected Mont Brouilly-area estates: accessible, medium-bodied and fruit-forward with the appellation's characteristic fresh cherry and clean mineral profile.

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Rustic

Domaine Jean-Claude LapaluBeaujolais€€€€€€€Still Red

Lapalu's cellar-selection Brouilly: deeper and more structured than the Vieilles Vignes, with darker fruit, herbal complexity and a savoury, mineral finish.

Château de la ChaizeBeaujolais€€€€€€€Still Red

Château de la Chaize's mid-tier Brouilly, longer-aged and more structured than the classique, with ripe fruit concentration and earthy Brouilly depth.

Domaine Jean-Claude LapaluBeaujolais€€€€€€€Still Red

Domaine des BureauxBeaujolais€€€€€€Still Red

★★★★★★★★★★

Domaine Jean-Marc DesprèsBeaujolais€€€€€€Still Red

Jean-Marc Desprès' Régnié from the youngest Beaujolais cru: fresh and approachable with characteristic raspberry and soft violet notes, medium tannin and bright finish.

Régnié Exsultet

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Rustic

Domaine Christian DucrouxBeaujolais€€€€€€€Still Red

Ducroux's prestige Régnié from Demeter-certified biodynamic farming: deep cherry-and-clay concentration, wild-yeast complexity and firm mineral structure.

Cuvée page →

Régnié Le Roc

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Domaine Christian DucrouxBeaujolais€€€€€€€Still Red

Le Roc ('the rock') is Ducroux's granite-soil Régnié: lighter and more mineral than Exsultet, showing the appellation's fragrant, crunchy cherry character with a distinctive rocky freshness.

Cuvée page →

Bauduc Les Trois Hectares Rouge

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Chateau BauducBordeaux€€€€€€Still Red

Bauduc's premium red from a single Bordeaux Superieur block: 75% Merlot, 25% Cabernet Sauvignon aged in small barrels for added structure. Anne Krebiehl MW describes it as juicy, plush, and beautifully open with oak-kissed fruit.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Lynch-Bages

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Rustic

Chateau Lynch-BagesBordeaux$$$$$$$$$Still Red

Lynch-Bages consistently overperforms its Fifth Growth classification. A richer, more extrovert Pauillac than the first-growths, with cassis generosity, smoky tobacco, and a robust structure that ages beautifully over 20-30 years.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Meyney

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Rustic

Chateau MeyneyBordeaux$$$$$$$Still Red

One of Saint-Estephe's most consistent and value-oriented estates, Meyney produces full-bodied, tannic wines with a distinctive spice and dark berry character. Notable for its high Petit Verdot content (10%) contributing structure and aromatic complexity.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Moulin Pey-Labrie

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Savoury

Chateau Moulin Pey-LabrieBordeaux€€€€€€€Still Red

The benchmark Canon-Fronsac red from 6.75 hectares of 65-year-average-age vines, farmed biodynamically and organically. Merlot with 5% Malbec produces a fragrant, perfumed wine with remarkable freshness for the Right Bank.

Cuvée page →

Chateau de la Riviere

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

Chateau de la RiviereBordeaux€€€€€€€Still Red

The flagship Fronsac estate red from 60 hectares on clay-limestone soils above the Dordogne. Merlot-led and supple with dark chocolate and ripe cherry; aged in spectacular 16th-century underground cellars at constant 13 degrees.

Cuvée page →

Château Chasse-Spleen

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Savoury

Château Chasse-SpleenBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still Red

The benchmark Cru Bourgeois Exceptionnel of Moulis: Cabernet-dominant with dark fruit, pencil lead and licorice, offering genuine structure and ageing potential well beyond its humble classification.

Cuvée page →

Château Larose-Trintaudon

3.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Château Larose-TrintaudonBordeaux€€€€€€€Still Red

The largest single vineyard in the Médoc produces an consistently approachable, fruit-forward Haut-Médoc with a well-balanced structure and excellent everyday drinkability at an accessible price.

Cuvée page →

Echo de Lynch-Bages

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Chateau Lynch-BagesBordeaux$$$$$$$$Still Red

Second wine of Lynch-Bages since 2007 (previously sold as Chateau Haut-Bages Averous). Higher Merlot component than the grand vin provides accessible fruit and earlier drinking; retains characteristic Lynch-Bages tobacco and cassis signature.

Cuvée page →

Fleur de Clinet

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Chateau ClinetBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still Red

The accessible second wine of Clinet from estate vines, made to reveal its qualities early. Smooth-textured, fruit-driven Pomerol Merlot with approachable tannins and genuine ageing potential without requiring long cellaring.

Cuvée page →

La Demoiselle de Sociando-Mallet

3.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Château Sociando-MalletBordeaux€€€€€€€Still Red

The Merlot-dominant second wine of Sociando-Mallet, offering earlier approachability with a more typically Médoc fruit profile than the austere and structured grand vin.

Cuvée page →

La Gravette de Certan

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Vieux Chateau CertanBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still Red

The accessible, Merlot-dominant second selection from Vieux Chateau Certan, made from younger vines and less-selected barrels. Forward, fruit-driven, and approachable early but retaining the estate's hallmark iron-mineral signature.

Cuvée page →

La Réserve de Léoville Barton

3.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Château Léoville BartonBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still Red

An elegant and approachable expression of Saint-Julien from younger vines, with a higher Merlot proportion giving early accessibility and soft ripe fruit.

Cuvée page →

Le Clementin du Pape Clement Rouge

3.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Chateau Pape ClementBordeaux€€€€€€€Still Red

The second red wine of Pape Clement since 1986. Merlot-dominant for approachability; round red cherry and currant fruit with cedar and soft spice notes. Designed for earlier drinking than the grand vin while reflecting the same Pessac terroir.

Cuvée page →

Le Pauillac de Chateau Latour

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Chateau LatourBordeaux$$$$$$$$Still Red

Third wine of Latour produced since 1989. Merlot-dominant and made primarily from young vines; developed for the restaurant market as a generously fruity, earlier-drinking Pauillac.

Cuvée page →

Les Hauts de Pontet-Canet

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Chateau Pontet-CanetBordeaux$$$$$$$$Still Red

Second wine of Pontet-Canet since 1982. Produced in limited quantities as Alfred Tesseron prefers lower yields and declassification over large second-wine production; earlier-drinking, approachable Pauillac from biodynamic-certified vineyards.

Cuvée page →

Les Hauts de Trintaudon

3.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Château Larose-TrintaudonBordeaux€€€€€€Still Red

The accessible second wine of Larose-Trintaudon, sourced from the same vines and terroir as the grand vin but crafted for early approachability and everyday enjoyment.

Cuvée page →

Mondot

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Chateau Troplong MondotBordeaux€€€€€€€Still Red

Second wine of Troplong Mondot, Merlot-dominant with emphasis on freshness and early drinkability. A juicy, earthy black cherry and spring flower profile with wet-stone limestone terroir character; approachable and unpretentious.

Cuvée page →

Prieur de Meyney

3.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Chateau MeyneyBordeaux$$$$$$$Still Red

Second wine of Chateau Meyney since 1979, named for the estate's 17th-century priory origins. Produced from younger vines (approximately 35-40 years) using minimal new oak to preserve fresh fruit character; a reliable everyday Saint-Estephe.

Cuvée page →

Domaine Rollin Pere et FilsBurgundy€€€€€€€Still Red

A firm, savoury village Aloxe-Corton with dark cherry, spice and earthy depth.

★★★★★★★★★★

Chartogne-Taillet Les Barres

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High acidity · Savoury · Rustic

Champagne Chartogne-TailletChampagne€€€€€€€€€Sparkling Traditional

A single-parcel varietal Pinot Meunier from ungrafted, own-rooted old vines: ripe orchard fruit and spice over smoke and white flowers, vinous and deep with a savoury length.

Cuvée page →

Drappier Carte d'Or Brut

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High acidity · Savoury · Rustic

Champagne DrappierChampagne€€€€€€€€Sparkling Traditional

Drappier's Pinot Noir-led Cote des Bar house brut: quince, red apple and spice over fresh brioche, with ripe Pinot fruit, a rounded texture and a long finish.

Cuvée page →

Drappier Quattuor Blanc de Quatre Blancs

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High acidity · Rustic · Savoury

Champagne DrappierChampagne€€€€€€€€Sparkling Traditional

A rare four-white-grape Champagne in equal parts: acacia honey, beeswax and marzipan giving way to quince jelly, raspberry jam and warm brioche, textural and exotic with fresh acidity.

Cuvée page →

Egly-Ouriet Les Vignes de Vrigny

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High acidity · Savoury · Rustic

Champagne Egly-OurietChampagne€€€€€€€€€Sparkling Traditional

A rare varietal Pinot Meunier from old Vrigny vines: ripe orchard fruit and spice over white flowers and smoke, fleshy and vinous with a savoury depth and long finish.

Cuvée page →

Bailén Oloroso Seco

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Bold · Rustic

OsborneJerez & Sherry€€€€€€Fortified Sherry

Osborne's classic dry Oloroso; full body from long oxidative solera ageing, dark dried-fruit and rancio complexity characteristic of El Puerto's Atlantic-moderated Oloroso style.

Cuvée page →

Oloroso Sangre y Trabajadero

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Bold · Rustic

Bodegas Gutiérrez ColosíaJerez & Sherry€€€€€€Fortified Sherry

Oloroso named after the Sangre y Trabajadero pago (Blood and Toil): full-bodied, dry and concentrated with the characteristic rancio depth of old El Puerto oxidative soleras.

Cuvée page →

Bartolo Mascarello Barbera d'Alba

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Savoury

Bartolo MascarelloPiedmont€€€€€€€€Still Red

A traditional, energetic Barbera from the same Barolo-village family known for understatement and balance rather than oak or extraction.

Ca' del Baio Barbaresco Vallegrande

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Elegant

Ca' del BaioPiedmont€€€€€€€€Still Red

A garnet Barbaresco with an intense bouquet of powerful fruit, dried flowers and underbrush, dry, smooth and rounded with a warm finish and balanced tannins.

Fontanafredda Barolo Serralunga d'Alba

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Savoury

FontanafreddaPiedmont€€€€€€€€Still Red

The classic village Barolo from the largest contiguous estate in the Langhe, blending organically farmed Serralunga fruit into a traditional, fairly priced Nebbiolo.

G.D. Vajra Langhe Freisa Kye

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Savoury

G.D. VajraPiedmont€€€€€€€€Still Red

Kye is Vajra's serious, oak-aged take on Freisa, a near-forgotten Nebbiolo relative, giving a tannic, high-toned red with wild-berry character.

Giacomo Conterno Barbera d'Alba Cascina Francia

★★★★★★★★★★

Giacomo ConternoPiedmont€€€€€€€€Still Red

A serious, age-worthy Barbera from the same Cascina Francia vineyard in Serralunga, given long ageing in large old barrel in the traditionalist Conterno manner.

Marchesi di BaroloPiedmont€€€€€€€€Still Red

The classic village Barolo of the house, blending fruit from across the Barolo commune for a structured, traditional and fairly priced Nebbiolo.

★★★★★★★★★★

Michele ChiarloPiedmont€€€€€€€€Still Red

From the La Court cru acquired in 1995, Chiarlo's flagship Barbera shows red fruit, mature cherry, raspberry and sweet tobacco over a full-bodied, harmonious palate with a savoury, round finish.

A Nizza DOCG Barbera from the La Court cru, bright ruby with red fruit, mature cherry, raspberry and sweet tobacco, full-bodied and harmonious with a savoury round finish.

Dry · Full-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Chateau d'AqueriaRhone Valley€€€€€€€Still Red

The red Lirac from Aqueria; good value, herb-driven, and fruit-forward with the estate's characteristic Provencal warmth. Lirac at its most accessible and traditional.

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Funky

Azienda Vinicola CalabrettaSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Calabretta's classic Etna Rosso: the blend of Nerello Mascalese and Nerello Cappuccio from century-old ungrafted vines, biodynamically farmed and made without additions, produces a wine of honest rustic charm and deep mineral character.

Cuvée page →

Calabretta Vigne Vecchie

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Contemplative

Azienda Vinicola CalabrettaSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Vigne Vecchie (old vines) showcases the depth of Calabretta's ungrafted Nerello: light in colour but profound in mineral detail, with the incense-and-iron character of century-old Etna vines.

Cuvée page →

Cantina Rallo Marsala Superiore

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Low acidity · Rustic · Festive

Cantina RalloSicily€€€€€€Fortified Marsala

Rallo's Marsala Superiore is the historic house's standard fortified expression: accessible, caramelised-fruit and gently oxidative from the 1860-founded Marsala cellar.

Cuvée page →

Cantine Milazzo Mamertino Rosso

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

Cantine MilazzoSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

One of the few expressions of the rare Mamertino DOC: a Nero d'Avola and Nocera blend from the Milazzo hills near the Tyrrhenian, with the dense dark-fruit character of Nero d'Avola lifted by Nocera's bitter herb notes.

Cuvée page →

Cantine Milazzo Nero d'Avola

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Cantine MilazzoSicily€€€€€€Still Red

Cantine Milazzo's Sicilia DOC Nero d'Avola from Tyrrhenian-influenced north-east Sicily: accessible, dark-fruited and an everyday ambassador for this native grape at a coastal terroir.

Cuvée page →

Cottanera Etna Rosso Calderara Sottana

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Rustic

CottaneraSicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

Cottanera's single-contrada Calderara Sottana Etna Rosso: firmer and more structured than the estate red, showcasing the depth of this historic north-slope contrada.

Cuvée page →

Cottanera Etna Rosso Contrada Diciassettesalme

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

CottaneraSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

The Cottanera Etna Rosso is the estate's primary north-slope red: approachable and fruit-forward, with Nerello's cherry character and Etna's herbaceous mineral finish.

Cuvée page →

Cusumano Benuara

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Bold · Rustic

CusumanoSicily€€€€€€Still Red

Cusumano's Nero d'Avola and Syrah blend from Sicilian estate vineyards: the pepper-spice of Syrah amplifies Nero d'Avola's dark fruit and the barrique treatment adds structure and warmth.

Donnafugata Sherazade

★★★★★★★★★★

DonnafugataSicily€€€€€€Still Red

Donnafugata's entry-level Nero d'Avola offers the grape's warm, plum-cherry character with the smooth, approachable style the estate is known for at this price point.

★★★★★★★★★★

Feudi del PisciottoSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Feudi del Pisciotto's DOCG expression from the restored manor estate in the Val di Noto shows the Cerasuolo di Vittoria character cleanly: cherry, mineral and herbaceous with the appellation's characteristic clay-soil earthiness.

★★★★★★★★★★

Feudo MontoniSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

The more approachable Feudo Montoni Nero d'Avola; less austere than Vrucara, with a cherry-herb character and the estate's distinctive high-altitude mineral warmth.

Firriato's entry-level Nero d'Avola under the Chiaramonte label is an accessible, fruit-forward expression of the variety: warm, rounded and designed for everyday enjoyment.

★★★★★★★★★★

Cantine FlorioSicily€€€€€€Fortified Marsala

Florio's accessible everyday Marsala Superiore Semisecco: the classic Marsala for cooking and casual drinking, with soft caramelised-fruit character and gentle oxidative notes.

Girolamo Russo San Lorenzo

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

Girolamo RussoSicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

San Lorenzo is the most immediately accessible of the Russo single-contrada range: fragrant, iron-streaked, with vibrant sour cherry and a saline volcanic freshness that makes it the most drinkable Russo Etna Rosso in its youth.

★★★★★★★★★★

GraciSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

The entry Graci Etna Rosso offers immediate pleasure: Nerello Mascalese at its most approachable - fragrant, herb-scented, with the estate's characteristic saline mineral finish.

Gulfi's entry-level single-contrada Nero d'Avola from the Ibleo plateau: concentrated dark fruit, carob and licorice with firm tannin and a warm volcanic-clay mineral finish.

Maggio ViniSicily€€€€€€Still Red

Maggio Vini's Nero d'Avola from the ultra-sun-drenched Maccari-Noto area offers one of the island's richest and warmest expressions of the variety: full-bodied, carob-dark-fruited with licorice depth.

Mandrarossa's varietal Nero d'Avola shows the cooperative's Menfi terroir at its most accessible: cherry-plum, herbal and warm, a reliable everyday Sicilian red with the typical coastal-influence freshness of Menfi.

MorganteSicily€€€€€€Still Red

Morgante's estate Nero d'Avola from the Agrigento interior shows the full-bodied, warm-fruited character of the grape from hot-climate inland soils, with approachable tannin and immediate appeal.

Murgo's estate Etna Rosso from the eastern slope of Etna: approachable and herb-scented, with the saline freshness characteristic of the Santa Venerina area.

Mofete Rosso is Palmento Costanzo's estate red: organically farmed Nerello Mascalese from the Santo Spirito contrada producing a fragrant, mineral-driven Etna Rosso with appealing freshness.

Ghiaia Nera (black gravel) is the benchmark cuvee of Tasca's Etna estate, taking its name from the black volcanic gravel of the Rampante contrada: mineral-driven, fragrant Nerello Mascalese from the north slope.

Tenuta Gatti Nero d'Avola

★★★★★★★★★★

Tenuta GattiSicily€€€€€€Still Red

Tenuta Gatti's Nero d'Avola from the Nebrodi slopes in the Messina province is unusually mineral and herbal compared to southern Sicilian examples: the altitude and Nebrodi scrubland influence give a cooler, more aromatic character to this northerly Nero d'Avola.

Sangiovese-led Chianti Classico Riserva in a juicier, more accessible Antinori register, with cherry and tobacco at the core.

Allegrini Palazzo della Torre

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

AllegriniVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

A Veronese IGT using partial ripasso over Amarone pomace adding richness to the Corvina-Rondinella blend, while a small Sangiovese component adds acidity. Approachable but complex mid-tier red from Allegrini.

Cuvée page →

Allegrini Valpolicella Classico Superiore

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

AllegriniVeneto€€€€€€Still Red

Allegrini's estate Classico Superiore reflects the house's high Corvinone proportion (45%) adding floral lift and softer tannin alongside Corvina's cherry-herb character; a bright, food-friendly everyday Valpolicella.

Cuvée page →

Bertani Valpolicella Ripasso

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

BertaniVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Bertani's Ripasso benefits from the estate's traditional Corvina-Rondinella blend and refermentation over dried Amarone skins, producing a mid-weight red with the freshness typical of the traditional house style.

Cuvée page →

Ca' La Bionda Amarone della Valpolicella Classico

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Contemplative

Ca' La BiondaVeneto€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Ca' La Bionda's Amarone reflects the volcanic and calcareous soils of the Marano valley top: a mineral, iron-inflected style with high acidity and a savoury character quite distinct from estates lower in the valley.

Cuvée page →

Ca' La Bionda Amarone della Valpolicella Classico Castel Caina

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Rustic

Ca' La BiondaVeneto€€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Castel Caina is Ca' La Bionda's premium single-vineyard selection from the highest Marano valley plots at the foot of the ancient Caina hill; the volcanic soils produce even greater mineral depth and acidity than the estate Classico, with outstanding ageing potential.

Cuvée page →

Cantina Valpolicella Negrar Amarone della Valpolicella Classico

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

Cantina Valpolicella NegrarVeneto€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

The cooperative's estate Amarone Classico provides accessible quality from 230 family growers across the Classico zone; a solid, tobacco-cherry traditional Amarone at significantly better value than single-estate bottlings at comparable quality.

Cuvée page →

Cantina Valpolicella Negrar Domaso Amarone della Valpolicella Classico

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Bold

Cantina Valpolicella NegrarVeneto€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Domaso is the cooperative's flagship single-vineyard Amarone from the 14-hectare Maternigo hill parcel, showing appreciably greater concentration and mineral precision than the estate-level wine; the earthy, tobacco-cedar style reflects the Maternigo terroir's calcareous character.

Cuvée page →

Cantine Campagnola Bardolino Classico DOC

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Cantine CampagnolaVeneto€€€€€€Still Red

One of Bardolino's oldest estates, founded in 1889. Campagnola's Bardolino Classico is a genuine expression of the pale ruby, low-tannin, cherry-scented style from the original lakefront Bardolino zone. The estate's daily opening policy makes it a popular stop on the Lake Garda wine tourist circuit.

Cuvée page →

Cantine Campagnola Valpolicella Classico DOC

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Cantine CampagnolaVeneto€€€€€€Still Red

Campagnola's Valpolicella Classico from the estate's hillside vineyards in the historic Classico zone. A traditional expression of the DOC: light in colour and body but with the characteristic Corvina sour cherry and almond character of the Classico hills.

Cuvée page →

Corte Gardoni Bardolino Classico DOC

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Corte GardoniVeneto€€€€€€Still Red

Corte Gardoni's Bardolino Classico is produced from Corvina-dominated vines on the southern Garda morainic soils. Light-bodied, pale ruby and fresh: a classic everyday Garda red suited to fish, vegetable dishes and outdoor drinking.

Cuvée page →

Corte Gardoni Le Fontane Garda Cabernet DOC

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Savoury · Rustic

Corte GardoniVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

The Le Fontane (the springs) red from Corte Gardoni is a Garda DOC Cabernet-based blend aged in oak. The Garda DOC permits Bordeaux varieties and the morainic soils of Valeggio sul Mincio give the Cabernet a mineral freshness that distinguishes it from warmer Veneto reds.

Cuvée page →

Corte Sant'Alda Soraigo Valpolicella Superiore

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Funky · Rustic

Corte Sant'AldaVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Soraigo shows what biodynamic Valpolicella Classico tastes like with minimal intervention: earthy, mineral, tart cherry with clay soil expressiveness and a savoury, food-friendly character that makes it a superb natural-wine discovery.

Cuvée page →

Giuseppe Quintarelli Valpolicella Classico Superiore

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Savoury

Giuseppe QuintarelliVeneto€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Quintarelli's entry-level Valpolicella is already a lesson in traditional winemaking: high-acid, savoury cherry fruit with dried-herb complexity and three years in large oak adding gentle structure.

Cuvée page →

Gorgo Merlot Garda DOC

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

GorgoVeneto€€€€€€Still Red

Gorgo's Garda DOC Merlot provides a red wine counterpart to the estate's primary Bianco di Custoza focus. Lake Garda's moderating effect on the climate produces a well-rounded Merlot with soft tannins and ripe fruit.

Cuvée page →

Guerrieri Rizzardi Bardolino Classico

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Guerrieri RizzardiVeneto€€€€€€Still Red

A classic light-bodied Bardolino Classico from the Lake Garda shoreline estates. The Classico denomination requires production from the original Bardolino historic core. Guerrieri Rizzardi, as one of the oldest estates in the zone, produces a benchmark expression of the pale ruby, cherry-scented style.

Cuvée page →

Guerrieri Rizzardi Pojega Bardolino Superiore

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Savoury

Guerrieri RizzardiVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Pojega is the top expression from Guerrieri Rizzardi's Bardolino range, bottled under the Bardolino Superiore DOCG. Oak ageing adds structure and complexity to the base Classico profile. Named for the Pojega wine museum located on the estate.

Cuvée page →

Italo Cescon Merlot Piave DOC

3.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Italo CesconVeneto€€€€€€Still Red

The Piave plain Merlot DOC from Italo Cescon is a classic Trevisan everyday red. Merlot on the alluvial Piave soils produces a plum-forward, medium-bodied wine with soft tannins suited to simple food pairings.

Cuvée page →

Italo Cescon Raboso Piave DOC

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Bold

Italo CesconVeneto€€€€€€Still Red

Raboso Piave is one of Italy's most ancient and austere red grapes, native to the Piave river plains in Treviso province. Italo Cescon has championed its rehabilitation since the 1960s. High tannin and acid give the wine its characteristic austerity, but serious age-worthiness.

Cuvée page →

Le Salette Amarone della Valpolicella Classico Classico

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Bold

Le SaletteVeneto€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Le Salette's estate Amarone Classico, from the high-altitude Fumane vineyards farmed by Monica Scamperle, shows the distinctive freshness and orange-peel note that characterise the estate's high-elevation fruit; a more approachable entry to the Le Salette style than the Pergole Vece.

Cuvée page →

Le Salette Amarone della Valpolicella Classico Pergole Vece

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Rustic

Le SaletteVeneto€€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Pergole Vece is from Le Salette's century-old pergola-trained Corvina vines at 450m elevation in Fumane, producing an Amarone of particular freshness and aromatic complexity; high altitude preserves acidity while old vines add concentration.

Cuvée page →

Le Salette Valpolicella Classico Superiore

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

Le SaletteVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Le Salette's Classico Superiore benefits from the high-altitude Fumane terroir that gives freshness and floral lift to the Corvina base; an elegant, food-friendly expression of the appellation.

Cuvée page →

Lorenzo Begali Amarone della Valpolicella Classico Monte Ca' Bianca

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Rustic

Lorenzo BegaliVeneto€€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Begali's Monte Ca' Bianca is a traditional-school Amarone from very old Corvina vines; the deep mineral iron note and high acidity set it apart, making it one of the most distinctive and food-friendly examples in the Classico zone.

Cuvée page →

Lorenzo Begali Valpolicella Classico Superiore

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

Lorenzo BegaliVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Begali's Valpolicella Classico Superiore from Ca' Bianca old vines shows the same mineral-iron freshness as the estate's Amarone in a lighter register; a food-friendly expression of traditional San Pietro in Cariano Corvina.

Cuvée page →

Masi Campofiorin Rosso del Veronese

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

Masi AgricolaVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Campofiorin pioneered the ripasso technique in 1964, passing fresh Valpolicella wine over dried Amarone grape skins to extract colour, richness and complexity. A seminal wine that created an entire style category.

Cuvée page →

Masi Costasera Amarone della Valpolicella Classico

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Bold

Masi AgricolaVeneto€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Masi's entry-level Classico Amarone is the house's most widely distributed wine and among the most accessible classic-zone bottlings, balancing dried-cherry concentration with a restrained herbaceous character and good structure.

Cuvée page →

Masi Toar Rosso Veronese IGT

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Rustic

Masi AgricolaVeneto€€€€€€€€Still Red

Masi's Toar is a 30% Oseleta-dominant Veronese IGT that deliberately showcases the rarely-used indigenous variety; partial drying of the Oseleta component adds concentration and the grape's characteristic dark spice and iron mineral character.

Cuvée page →

Nicolis Amarone della Valpolicella Classico

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Bold

Nicolis Angelo e FigliVeneto€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

The Nicolis family's Amarone from the Villa Girardi estate in San Pietro in Cariano reflects a traditional, large-cask aged style with tobacco-leather character and solid structure; consistent quality across multiple vintages.

Cuvée page →

Nicolis Valpolicella Classico Superiore

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

Nicolis Angelo e FigliVeneto€€€€€€Still Red

The Nicolis estate Classico Superiore offers an honest, traditional Valpolicella from the Villa Girardi hillside; cherry-tobacco character with the freshness typical of a classic-zone Corvina aged gently in large casks.

Cuvée page →

Santa Sofia Amarone della Valpolicella Classico

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Elegant

Santa SofiaVeneto€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Santa Sofia's Amarone is a traditional-style Classico from the historic Pedemonte estate, emphasising tobacco-leather complexity and long cellarability; the 16th-century villa setting adds heritage gravitas to a consistently well-made Amarone.

Cuvée page →

Santa Sofia Valpolicella Classico Superiore

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Santa SofiaVeneto€€€€€€Still Red

Santa Sofia's Classico Superiore is a reliable, food-friendly, accessible Valpolicella from the historic Pedemonte estate with cherry-tobacco character and good acidity.

Cuvée page →

Serafini and Vidotto Phigaia

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Serafini and VidottoVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Phigaia is the accessible second wine from Serafini and Vidotto, allowing the estate to express the Montello terroir at a lower price point. The Cabernet Franc and Merlot blend is lighter, fresher and more immediately enjoyable than Il Rosso dell'Abazia.

Cuvée page →

Speri Amarone della Valpolicella Classico

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Elegant

Speri ViticoltoriVeneto€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Speri's estate Amarone Classico reflects the six-generation family's traditional approach with large-cask ageing, producing a fresh, floral, tobacco-inflected Amarone that remains one of the most consistent classic-zone expressions.

Cuvée page →

Speri Valpolicella Classico Superiore La Roverina

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

Speri ViticoltoriVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Speri's La Roverina Classico Superiore with partial ripasso technique; fresh cherry and tobacco character from the traditional large-cask approach, with added concentration from the ripasso step and the estate's characteristic acidity.

Cuvée page →

Tedeschi Amarone della Valpolicella Classico La Fabriseria

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Rustic

Tedeschi ViniVeneto€€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

La Fabriseria (old Venetian dialect for the forge) is Tedeschi's second single-vineyard Amarone from a plot on the Monte Olmi hillside; greater tobacco-iron concentration than the Capitel Monte Olmi, with a more austere, mineral-forward character.

Cuvée page →

Tedeschi Valpolicella Classico Superiore Capitel dei Nicalo

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

Tedeschi ViniVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

The Capitel dei Nicalo is Tedeschi's Valpolicella Superiore partially ripassoed over dried skins, offering accessible Corvina cherry fruit with tobacco structure and good food-pairing versatility.

Cuvée page →

Tenuta Sant'Antonio Amarone della Valpolicella Campo dei Gigli

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Rustic

Tenuta Sant'AntonioVeneto€€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

The 20% Oseleta in Campo dei Gigli adds a peppery, dark-spice dimension to the Corvina-Corvinone dried fruit base; from the Mezzane valley outside the Classico zone, the wine shows an earthier, more structured style than comparable Classico Amarone.

Cuvée page →

Tenuta Sant'Antonio Valpolicella Superiore Ripasso

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

Tenuta Sant'AntonioVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Tenuta Sant'Antonio's Ripasso shows the Mezzane valley's characteristic earthiness alongside cherry-tobacco fruit; the small Oseleta addition adds a distinguishing spice note that sets it apart from Classico-zone examples.

Cuvée page →

Tommasi Amarone della Valpolicella Classico

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Bold

Tommasi ViticoltoriVeneto€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Tommasi's estate Amarone Classico represents the fourth-generation family's traditional approach: a high proportion of Corvinone adds floral lift to the dried-cherry concentration while three years in large casks develops structure.

Cuvée page →

Tommasi Valpolicella Classico Superiore Ripasso

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

Tommasi ViticoltoriVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Tommasi's Ripasso offers the hallmark refermentation complexity over dried Amarone skins in a well-structured, cherry-tobacco dominated medium red that is one of the zone's best-value offerings in the Ripasso DOC.

Cuvée page →

Valentina Cubi Amarone della Valpolicella Classico Morar

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Funky

Valentina CubiVeneto€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Valentina Cubi's certified organic Amarone from the Fumane hillsides shows a distinctly earthy, mineral character from organic viticulture; the Oseleta addition contributes pepper spice and the high acidity reflects the altitude of the Fumane Classico zone.

Cuvée page →

Valentina Cubi Valpolicella Classico Superiore

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Funky

Valentina CubiVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Valentina Cubi's organic Valpolicella Superiore shows the freshness of high-altitude Fumane fruit with earthy mineral character from organic farming; a genuinely characterful everyday Valpolicella with better-than-average expressiveness.

Cuvée page →

Vignalta Cabernet Colli Euganei Rosso

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Savoury · Rustic

VignaltaVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

A Cabernet-focused red from the volcanic slopes of the Euganean Hills. The Colli Euganei DOC allows Cabernet varieties and Vignalta's version shows the distinctive mineral thread of volcanic soil combined with the classic Cabernet Franc herbal character.

Cuvée page →

Viviani Amarone della Valpolicella Classico Campo Morar

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Rustic

VivianiVeneto€€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Campo Morar (meadow of mulberries) is Viviani's single-vineyard Amarone from the highest plots on the Mazzano ridge in Negrar, at over 400 metres elevation; the altitude confers remarkable freshness and acidity to the dried-fruit concentration, producing a wine of particular precision and longevity.

Cuvée page →

Viviani Valpolicella Classico Superiore

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Refreshing

VivianiVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Viviani's Classico Superiore from the high Mazzano ridge shows the altitude freshness that characterises all the estate's wines: iron mineral, tart cherry and mountain herbs in a lean, food-loving medium-red.

Cuvée page →

Zenato Amarone della Valpolicella Classico

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Rustic

ZenatoVeneto€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Zenato's Classico Amarone incorporates 20% Oseleta adding firm pepper tannin and dark fruit spice to the Corvina-led blend; aged in a combination of large Slavonian and small French oak that bridges traditional and modern styles.

Cuvée page →

Zenato Valpolicella Superiore Ripassa

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

ZenatoVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Zenato's Ripassa shows the Oseleta grape's peppery-spice influence even at the mid-tier level: good concentration from refermentation over dried skins with a distinguishing warmth from the native Oseleta component.

Cuvée page →
